front: Short Story: Boris Eikhenbaum coined 'skaz' in an essay about this story; called its narrator 'a performer, almost a comedian'
Short Story: the 'Person of Consequence' whose system is 'Strictness, strictness, and always strictness!' berates a clerk who then dies of fever
Short Story: a ghost haunts the Kalinkin Bridge stealing coats; a watchman sees a phantom with an 'enormous mustache'
Play: ends with a 'silent scene' where all characters freeze for a minute and a half; 'What are you laughing about? You are laughing at yourselves!'
Play: Khlestakov claims to be the author of The Marriage of Figaro; flirts with the Governor's wife and daughter
Play: Meyerhold directed a landmark 1926 production with actors emerging from 11 coffin-like doors during a bribe scene
Play: judge Lyapkin-Tyapkin keeps hunting equipment in his courtroom; comic squires Bobchinsky and Dobchinsky appear
Novel: the first part ends addressing 'O Rus'' and comparing the nation's 'headlong motion' to a troika
Novel: the protagonist gambles with the cheating Nozdryov while collecting rights to deceased serfs
Novel: the second volume was burned by the author before his death by self-starvation; ends mid-sentence with an anti-corruption speech
Short Story: a witch instructs Petro to decapitate a boy to obtain treasure beneath a blooming red fern
work: Evenings on a Farm Near Dikanka

front: Short Story: the blacksmith Vakula woos the vain Oksana by getting the Tsarina's slippers; the devil hides in coal sacks
work: Evenings on a Farm Near Dikanka

front: Short Story: Poprishchin declares himself King of Spain (Ferdinand VII) in erratically-dated letters; the dogs Meggy and Fidel are overheard gossiping
Short Story: ends 'Mother, mother, have pity on your sick child! And do you know that the Bey of Algiers has a wart under his nose?'
Short Story: the title object is found in a loaf of bread by the barber Ivan Yakovlevich; later seen wearing a gold-braided uniform heading to Riga
Short Story: the painter Piskarev takes opium and slits his throat after the woman he followed turns out to be a prostitute; contrasted with Lieutenant Pirogov
Short Story: cobbler Hoffmann and tinsmith Schiller beat up Pirogov for dancing with Schiller's wife
Short Story: the philosophy student Khoma chants prayers for three nights over a witch's coffin; the title gnome has eyelids that touch the floor
Short Story: a painter finds money in the frame of a cursed portrait of a moneylender; later becomes a monk painting scenes with demonic eyes
Novel: the title Cossack tells his son Andriy 'I gave you life, I will also kill you!'; tied to a tree and burned alive
Play: Podkolyosin jumps out a window to avoid the title event; a man named Yaichnitsa ('omelette') courts Agafya
Play: a deck of cards is called 'Adelaida Ivanovna'; the protagonist is tricked by 'the Glovs'
Short Story: a hungover man who promised to host dinner is found by angry guests hiding in the title vehicle
Author: Tommaso Landolfi wrote a bizarre story in which a fictional version of this author marries a balloon named Caracas that contracts syphilis
Author: Belinsky wrote a harsh letter denouncing Selected Passages from Correspondence with My Friends; this author later died of self-starvation with leeches on his nose
